attribute_space.course_code: Exact course identifier used in the target review domain.
attribute_space.semester: Term marker such as Fall 2022 or Spring 2023.
attribute_space.student_background: Prior familiarity with the topic, math maturity, coding fluency, or security/network background.
attribute_space.motivation: Why the student took the course, such as requirement, curiosity, or specialization fit.
attribute_space.grade_band: Observed or expected performance level, including ambiguity around final grade when natural.
attribute_space.workload_intensity: Perceived weekly effort and project crunch level.
attribute_space.assessment_profile: Relative emphasis on projects, exams, reports, quizzes, or participation.
attribute_space.instruction_quality: Perception of lectures, notes, examples, and conceptual clarity.
attribute_space.support_channel_experience: Office hours, forums, TAs, Piazza, email responsiveness, or peer support.
attribute_space.administrative_friction: Confusing rubrics, grading opacity, deadlines, tooling issues, or policy frustration.
attribute_space.emotional_temperature: Measured, mixed, enthusiastic, bitter, exhausted, or resigned tone.
attribute_space.linguistic_style: Analytic, terse, rant-like, uncertain, casually compressed, or moderately polished prose.
attribute_space.hedging_and_uncertainty: Degree of confidence, second-guessing, or partial disagreement inside the same review.
attribute_space.specificity_markers: Realistic concrete references to assignments, lectures, datasets, readings, forums, or deadlines.
attribute_space.recommendation_stance: Would recommend, only for prepared students, or actively discourage.

prompt_cycles.name: rich_attributes_baseline
prompt_cycles.goal: Generate domain-matched reviews using a larger attribute space while preserving aspect-level labels.
prompt_cycles.additional_instruction: Write a realistic first-person course review with uneven detail. Avoid textbook sentiment wording, avoid obvious label leakage, and keep the tone consistent with the sampled student persona.

prompt_cycles.name: reduce_synthetic_signatures
prompt_cycles.goal: Suppress robotic balance and over-explicit contrasts that make synthetic text easy to detect.
prompt_cycles.additional_instruction: Use partial contradictions, hedging, and one or two concrete course-specific details. Do not summarize every aspect neatly. Let some judgments feel incidental rather than checklist-like.

prompt_cycles.name: messier_realism
prompt_cycles.goal: Increase natural unevenness and domain realism without collapsing label faithfulness.
prompt_cycles.additional_instruction: Allow sentence fragments, imperfect transitions, asymmetric emphasis, grade uncertainty, and references to forums, projects, grading confusion, or time pressure when appropriate. Keep the text plausible for a real student review.

recommended_protocol.step_1: Sample a matched set of real reviews from the public OMSCS source.
recommended_protocol.step_2: Generate synthetic reviews with cycle 0 using the richer attribute space.
recommended_protocol.step_3: Ask the judge model to label each blind item as real or synthetic and provide confidence.
recommended_protocol.step_4: Inspect false positives and false negatives for synthetic signatures.
recommended_protocol.step_5: Refine the prompt and rerun cycles 1 and 2.
recommended_protocol.step_6: Stop once the judge is near chance or once prompt changes start harming label control.

evaluation_targets.secondary: Judge confidence gap between real and synthetic items
evaluation_targets.tertiary: Qualitative reasons the judge gives for calling a review synthetic
